title: centaurea × ephelidea
intro: Centaurea × ephelidea, also known as Mountain Cornflower, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, resulting from the cross between Centaurea montana and Centaurea jacea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Centaurea × ephelidea has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, offering relief from pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of the plant, including infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
